source_jobs.title: Principal Solution Engineer (824)
source_jobs.description:
At Protegrity, we lead innovation by using AI and quantum-resistant cryptography to transform data protection across cloud-native, hybrid, on-premises, and open source environments. We leverage advanced cryptographic methods such as tokenization, format-preserving encryption, and quantum-resilient techniques to protect sensitive data. As a global leader in data security, our mission is to ensure that data isn’t just valuable but also usable, trusted, and safe.



Protegrity offers the opportunity to work at the intersection of innovation and collaboration, with the ability to make a meaningful impact on the industry while working alongside some of the brightest minds. Together, we are redefining how the world safeguards data, enabling organizations to thrive in a GenAI era where data is the ultimate currency. If you're ready to shape the future of data security, Protegrity is the place for you.



Protegrity is seeking a passionate and talented Principal Solution Engineer to join our global Solution Engineering team in Australia.



Role Overview

This job is for you if, as a Principal Solution Engineer, you thrive at the intersection of technology and business value, working closely with enterprise customers and internal teams to define, design, and deliver tailored data protection solutions.



Your day will involve engaging with customer stakeholders from C-level executives to technical teams to uncover critical requirements, demonstrate Protegrity’s value, and lead proof of concept initiatives.



You’ll architect secure, scalable solutions that align with customer goals while serving as their trusted technical advisor throughout the sales lifecycle. In this role, you’ll collaborate with Account Executives, Product Management, and Professional Services, contribute to technical strategy, and mentor peers all while navigating a dynamic, fast paced environment that values innovation, autonomy, and continuous improvement.



Your success will be shaped by your ability to listen deeply, communicate clearly, and apply a structured, consultative approach to solving complex data protection challenges.



What you’ll do

 - Conduct in-depth discovery, data-flow mapping, and documentation of customer environments, both on-site and remotely.

 - Deliver compelling technical presentations and product demonstrations to stakeholders including C-level executives, developers, DBAs, IT security, and other technical or business audiences.

 - Lead and manage Proof-of-Concepts, contribute to RFI/RFP responses, and validate customer requirements through hands-on technical engagement.

 - Architect, install, and configure Protegrity software solutions tailored to customer needs and deployment environments.

 - Create detailed solution design and project planning documents using standardized formats.

 - Collaborate with sales teams, partners, and professional services to align technical activities with customer outcomes.

 - Mentor other Solution Engineers and Services personnel while continuing to build your own expertise through certifications, hands-on labs, and current knowledge of data privacy and cybersecurity trends.

 - Regularly use cloud labs across AWS, Azure, and GCP to validate capabilities, support customer scenarios, and maintain accurate records in CRM and other presales applications.



What you’ll need

 - Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience, such as 4+ years in a similar technical role.

 - 8+ years of experience in solution engineering, consulting, presales, or technical advisory roles across data, security, privacy, AI, or adjacent enterprise software domains.

 - Demonstrated success leading technical engagements in complex enterprise environments, from discovery and architecture through technical validation and executive alignment.

 - Solid understanding of enterprise architecture, cloud environments including AWS, Azure, and GCP, hybrid deployment models, and modern containerized architectures.

 - Str

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager, Program Management (823)
source_jobs.description:
At Protegrity, we lead innovation by using AI and quantum-resistant cryptography to transform data protection across cloud-native, hybrid, on-premises, and open source environments. We leverage advanced cryptographic methods such as tokenization, format-preserving encryption, and quantum-resilient techniques to protect sensitive data. As a global leader in data security, our mission is to ensure that data isn’t just valuable but also usable, trusted, and safe.



Protegrity offers the opportunity to work at the intersection of innovation and collaboration, with the ability to make a meaningful impact on the industry while working alongside some of the brightest minds. Together, we are redefining how the world safeguards data, enabling organizations to thrive in a GenAI era where data is the ultimate currency. If you're ready to shape the future of data security, Protegrity is the place for you.



Role Overview – Why This Role Matters

The Senior Manager, Program Management leads the delivery of strategic initiatives across Protegrity’s product and platform portfolio, ensuring predictable execution and alignment with business objectives.



This role partners closely with Product, Engineering, Security, and Executive Leadership to drive cross-functional programs, improve delivery maturity, and provide visibility into portfolio performance.



The position plays a critical role in scaling program management practices while enabling innovation across cloud, data security, and AI data security solutions.



What You’ll Do:

 - Lead the Program Management function and establish scalable governance, delivery frameworks, and operating practices that improve organizational effectiveness.

 - Drive execution of strategic product, platform, and business initiatives, ensuring alignment between roadmap priorities and delivery outcomes.

 - Partner with Product Management, Engineering, Security, and Operations teams to coordinate cross-functional planning, execution, and release activities.

 - Manage portfolio-level risks, dependencies, resource constraints, and trade-offs to support predictable and high-quality delivery.

 - Provide executive leadership with clear visibility into portfolio health, delivery performance, key metrics, and strategic recommendations.

 - Lead governance and delivery processes for AI, GenAI, and intelligent automation initiatives, ensuring operational readiness and measurable business impact.

 - Champion continuous improvement initiatives that enhance delivery predictability, operational excellence, and program management maturity.

 - Coach, develop, and lead a team of Program Managers while fostering a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous learning.



What You’ll Need:

 - 10+ years of program or project management experience within enterprise software, SaaS, cybersecurity, or technology organizations.

 - 5+ years of people leadership experience managing Program Managers or related delivery professionals.

 - Experience leading complex, cross-functional programs across Product Management, Engineering, Security, and Operations teams.

 - Proven success building, scaling, or transforming Program Management or PMO organizations.

 - Strong understanding of Agentic AI-driven SDLC, DevOps, and program management processes, with experience establishing governance, workflows, and accountability models that integrate human teams, intelligent agents, and automation across planning, execution, release management, and continuous improvement.

 - Experience managing portfolio governance, risk management, capacity planning, and executive stakeholder communications.

 - Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Engineering, Business, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.

 - Strong analytical, communication, and leadership skills with the ability to influence decisions across all levels of the organization.



Nice to Have:

 - Experience with data security, encryption, tokenization,

source_jobs.title: Principal Software Engineer - Data Discovery (GenAI) (812)
source_jobs.description:
At Protegrity, we lead innovation by using AI and quantum-resistant cryptography to transform data protection across cloud-native, hybrid, on-premises, and open source environments. We leverage advanced cryptographic methods such as tokenization, format-preserving encryption, and quantum-resilient techniques to protect sensitive data. As a global leader in data security, our mission is to ensure that data isn’t just valuable but also usable, trusted, and safe.



Protegrity offers the opportunity to work at the intersection of innovation and collaboration, with the ability to make a meaningful impact on the industry while working alongside some of the brightest minds. Together, we are redefining how the world safeguards data, enabling organizations to thrive in a GenAI era where data is the ultimate currency. If you're ready to shape the future of data security, Protegrity is the place for you.



As a Principal Software Engineer on our Data Discovery team, you will design, build, and evolve the backend services, distributed systems, and GenAI-powered capabilities at the core of our platform. This is a hands-on, senior individual-contributor role for an engineer who owns large systems end to end, from architecture and implementation through to scale, performance, and operation in production.



The systems you build need to be fast, scalable, and reliable under heavy load. We’re looking for engineers with strong instincts for distributed systems and performance, who care about clean design and take genuine ownership of how their software behaves once it ships.



You will work across the full lifecycle of the systems you own, with a strong focus on building software that is correct, reliable, and maintainable at scale.



Responsibilities:

 - Design, build, and own the backend services and distributed systems behind our data discovery platform.

 - Lead the architecture of complex, high-scale systems, balancing performance, reliability, and maintainability.

 - Keep systems fast and reliable as data volumes and traffic grow.

 - Develop and operate cost-efficient services on major cloud platforms.

 - Write clean, production-grade code that sets a strong example for the team.

 - Build software that is testable by design, with strong automated coverage as part of normal delivery.

 - Drive the reliability and observability of the services you run.

 - Investigate and resolve difficult issues across services and environments, getting to the root cause.

 - Apply GenAI and AI-assisted techniques both to build new product features and to speed up your own engineering workflow.

 - Partner with engineers and other teams on design and architecture decisions, raising the technical bar across the organization.

 - Mentor senior and mid-level engineers and help strengthen the team’s technical depth.

Qualifications:

 - 8+ years of professional software engineering experience building and operating production backend and distributed systems.

 - Strong programming skills (Python preferred) with a track record of shipping high-quality software as a core developer.

 - Deep experience designing and scaling distributed systems and APIs, with sound judgment on architecture and engineering trade-offs.

 - Demonstrated skill in designing for performance and scalability in high-throughput, data-intensive systems.

 - Experience contributing to strong quality practices, including effective testing, test automation, and collaboration with QA or release processes to help ensure reliable software delivery.

 - A habit of building testing and automation into your work from the start.

 - Solid understanding of CI/CD pipelines and the practices behind continuous, confident delivery.

 - Hands-on experience with Docker, Kubernetes, and Helm.

 - Experience running services in a major cloud environment (AWS, GCP, or Azure).

 - Comfortable debugging and troubleshooting complex multi-service systems in production.

 - Strong grasp of engineering bes

source_jobs.title: Principal Account Executive (813)
source_jobs.description:
At Protegrity, we lead innovation by using AI and quantum-resistant cryptography to transform data protection across cloud-native, hybrid, on-premises, and open source environments. We leverage advanced cryptographic methods such as tokenization, format-preserving encryption, and quantum-resilient techniques to protect sensitive data. As a global leader in data security, our mission is to ensure that data isn’t just valuable but also usable, trusted, and safe.



Protegrity offers the opportunity to work at the intersection of innovation and collaboration, with the ability to make a meaningful impact on the industry while working alongside some of the brightest minds. Together, we are redefining how the world safeguards data, enabling organizations to thrive in a GenAI era where data is the ultimate currency. If you're ready to shape the future of data security, Protegrity is the place for you.



Role Overview



As an Account Executive, you will be responsible for a defined territory of customers and prospects, creating and executing a go-to-market plan, leveraging a variety of resources (e.g., BDRs, Partners, Product Specialists, Solution Consultants, etc.)



By focusing on enabling our customer’s success and helping them achieve their desired business outcomes, you will become their trusted advisor responsible for extending the Protegrity footprint. Success in this role will be driven from a high level of curiosity and a structured approach to building new relationships, creating value for our customers, and ultimately booking new business.



What You’ll Do:

 - Identify and interpret our clients/prospects business objectives, build and lead virtual internal teams to create and accurately present complex solutions in an informative and easily understood fashion, either face to face or in virtual meetings

 - Develop both individual account & territory plan and drive new logo growth with the highest level of client satisfaction, while focusing on retention and renewals.

 - Achievement of assigned quotas in accordance with company goals, strategy, standards and policies. Clearly communicate goals for the accounts by business unit and develop effective strategies to meet client and company goals.

 - Accurately identify problems, recognize symptoms, and formulate effective solutions and alternatives.

 - Provide market and "voice of customer" feedback to the cross-functional teams via customer feedback and quarterly business reviews to flush out client needs to enhance product and solution development.

 - Partner with Marketing to develop account-based marketing strategies for accounts and publish client success stories.

 - Manage a number of existing Enterprise accounts.



What You’ll Need:

 - 10+ years of experience in client-facing enterprise sales, including cloud technologies and Generative AI concepts

 - Experience working directly and with our partner ecosystem. (AWS, Microsoft, Accenture, etc.) on behalf of customers.

 - Experience driving successful onboarding, expansion and renewal of existing customers, along with an understanding of large, complex customers and the ability to partner and influence the agendas of multiple client stakeholders.

 - Proven negotiation skills, at the C-suite level, with large customer organizations.

 - Ability to drive urgency in the sales cycle and execution of solution strategies; and an exceptional ability to manage a robust pipeline.

 - Excellent time management and organizational skills, ability to prioritize and use time effectively and efficiently

 - Demonstrated ability to drive significant revenue growth with highly competitive strategic clients.

 - Superior skills and ability to present and communicate effectively with C-level executives, both internally and externally



Nice to have:

 - Knowledge of Data Protection, Privacy and Security

 - Knowledge Software Sales, SAAS.

 - Industry expertise in: Financial Services, Health Care, Retail
   
   

We offer a co

source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative (811)
source_jobs.description:
At Protegrity, we lead innovation by using AI and quantum-resistant cryptography to transform data protection across cloud-native, hybrid, on-premises, and open source environments. We leverage advanced cryptographic methods such as tokenization, format-preserving encryption, and quantum-resilient techniques to protect sensitive data. As a global leader in data security, our mission is to ensure that data isn’t just valuable but also usable, trusted, and safe.



Protegrity offers the opportunity to work at the intersection of innovation and collaboration, with the ability to make a meaningful impact on the industry while working alongside some of the brightest minds. Together, we are redefining how the world safeguards data, enabling organizations to thrive in a GenAI era where data is the ultimate currency. If you're ready to shape the future of data security, Protegrity is the place for you.



About the Role

Protegrity’s Business Development team is growing, & we’re looking for a driven Business Development Representative (BDR) to help fuel pipeline across our Enterprise & Mid-Market sales teams.



In this role, you’ll focus on identifying, engaging, & qualifying prospective customers through strategic outbound & inbound efforts. You’ll partner closely with Sales & Marketing to generate high-quality opportunities & contribute directly to revenue growth.



This is an ideal opportunity for rising sales professionals looking to accelerate their careers, who thrive in a fast-paced, B2B technology environment & are eager to build a foundation in cybersecurity, data protection, & AI-driven innovation.





Key Responsibilities

Prospecting & Pipeline Generation

 - Generate new business opportunities with prospective Enterprise & Mid-Market clients.

 - Research target accounts, map buying groups, & identify key stakeholders.

 - Execute structured multi-channel outreach (phone, email, LinkedIn, marketing campaigns, event follow-up).

 - Qualify inbound & outbound leads & schedule discovery meetings for Account Executives.

 - Maintain accurate activity, pipeline, & account data within CRM systems.

 - Meet & exceed KPIs related to outreach activity, meetings booked, & pipeline contribution.
   
   

Collaboration & Execution

 - Partner with Sales & Marketing to align on campaigns, messaging, & target accounts.

 - Support follow-up on marketing campaigns, events, & inbound interest with defined SLAs.

 - Provide feedback on messaging, objections, & market trends to improve outreach effectiveness.

 - Participate in team training, call coaching, & ongoing enablement programs.
   
   

Continuous Improvement

 - Test & refine outreach strategies (messaging, personas, channels) to improve performance.

 - Stay current on Protegrity’s solutions, industry trends, & competitive landscape.

 - Contribute insights to team playbooks & best practices.



Qualifications & Experience

 - 3+ years of experience in sales, business development, or customer-facing roles (B2B tech preferred).

 - Experience in cybersecurity, data protection, cloud, or AI/analytics markets OR degrees or certifications in AI or Cybersecurity.

 - Excellent written & verbal communication skills.

 - Ability to manage time, prioritize tasks, & stay organized in a high-activity environment.

 - Coachable mindset with a willingness to learn & adapt quickly.

 - Experience with CRM tools (Salesforce preferred) & sales engagement platforms (SalesLoft or similar) is a plus.

 - Competitive, self-motivated, & driven to exceed targets.

 - Positive attitude & high energy.



What Success Looks Like (First 90 Days)

 - Consistently hitting activity & meeting targets.

 - Confidently executing outbound across multiple channels.

 - Generating qualified pipeline aligned to Sales priorities.

 - Demonstrating strong product & industry understanding.

 - Contributing to team momentum, improvement, & morale.



Should you accept this position, you will be required to consen

Protegrity is seeking a passionate and talented Senior Manager of Engineering to join our Engineering organization. This position is a remote role.



This job is for you if

You enjoy leading teams that ship customer-facing product experiences at scale. You bring strong front-end engineering depth, pair it with pragmatic people leadership, and love building reusable platform capabilities that help multiple product teams move faster. In this role you’ll lead 2–3 product teams (including the core UI team), set technical direction for a micro-frontend architecture and shell SDK, and stay close to the code—especially when a problem needs hands-on help. You’ll also champion AI-assisted development practices to accelerate delivery while keeping quality, security, and accessibility bar-high.



Responsibilities

 - Lead 2–3 product engineering teams (including the core UI team), providing day-to-day management, coaching, mentoring, and performance support while maintaining a strong technical presence.

 - Set technical direction for the UI platform: micro-frontend architecture, React/TypeScript standards, shell SDK interfaces, module contracts, and shared design-system/theming patterns.

 - Drive engineering execution: sprint planning, technical prioritization, code reviews, and hands-on contributions to unblock delivery and improve reliability.

 - Establish and evolve CI/CD for independently deployable front-end modules, including build pipelines, artifact management, automated testing, and safe rollout practices.

 - Ensure reusable UI and service capabilities are well-designed and documented (including company-wide ‘skills files’/patterns), and guide integration with supporting backend services such as user management and authorization.

 - Partner closely with Product Management to define roadmap, scope features, and deliver high-impact product outcomes.

 - Define and maintain clear module contracts, API descriptors, and SDK interfaces to enable independent development across teams.

 - Promote scalable integration patterns including event-driven communication and shared state approaches across modules and services.

source_jobs.title: Technical Lead (774)
source_jobs.description:
At Protegrity, we lead innovation by using AI and quantum-resistant cryptography to transform data protection across cloud-native, hybrid, on-premises, and open source environments. We leverage advanced cryptographic methods such as tokenization, format-preserving encryption, and quantum-resilient techniques to protect sensitive data. As a global leader in data security, our mission is to ensure that data isn’t just valuable but also usable, trusted, and safe.



Protegrity offers the opportunity to work at the intersection of innovation and collaboration, with the ability to make a meaningful impact on the industry while working alongside some of the brightest minds. Together, we are redefining how the world safeguards data, enabling organizations to thrive in a GenAI era where data is the ultimate currency. If you're ready to shape the future of data security, Protegrity is the place for you.



The global data privacy software market is projected to grow from $2.36 billion in 2022 to $25.85 billion by 2029. So, if you are searching for an innovative career, then the cybersecurity industry might just be the one for you. Protegrity, an international provider of data protection solutions, is looking for new team members who want to make an impact in a company that prioritizes something we all care about: our privacy.



Protegrity is seeking a passionate and talented Technical Lead to join our team.



We need people who recognize the opportunity, can plan effectively, take action and execute in the enterprise marketplace with our intuitive, diverse, and future-forward global teams. So, are you up for collaborating with the best in data security?



Join us on this journey and make an impact with one of the top 25 global software providers. We look forward to making our world become a better place with you on our team.



This job is for you if:

 - You are a dedicated and accomplished Technical Lead invited to join our team. In this role, you will assume responsibility for guiding the development of the Protegrity platform, ensuring strict adherence to architectural standards, and providing expert technical leadership across multiple teams. Success in this capacity requires exceptional collaboration with architects and senior engineers, as well as the ability to advocate for innovative design principles and foster a culture of technical excellence throughout our product teams.

 - This position is ideally suited for experienced professionals who excel at developing robust technological solutions and are motivated to oversee the complete end-to-end product delivery lifecycle.

 - Eligible candidates should possess a minimum of six years of relevant experience in the design, development, and integration of application software, including a proven track record of leading technical teams. Applicants are expected to demonstrate versatility in programming, exhibit comprehensive knowledge of software design concepts, and remain informed about the latest advancements in technology.



Responsibilities:

 - Leverage advanced technical leadership capabilities and substantial industry experience to design, implement, and uphold best practices, offering trusted counsel to promote sustainable, long-term growth while cultivating highly credible relationships with all stakeholders.

 - Remain at the forefront of industry and technological best practices and trends, with specific expertise in microservices architecture, Docker, Kubernetes, and other cloud technologies like AWS, GCP, Azure, to deliver value-added solutions.

 - Adaptability to learn and enhance skills in the domain of data security alongside the product’s technical environment (Linux, hardware etc.)

 - Participate in development and implementation of product specifications in conjunction with Product Management.

 - Pay attention to feasibility, system/feature integration and performance expectations.

 - Work with stakeholders in the company (E.g. Product Management, Archit

source_jobs.title: Associate Technical Writer (810)
source_jobs.description:
At Protegrity, we lead innovation by using AI and quantum-resistant cryptography to transform data protection across cloud-native, hybrid, on-premises, and open source environments. We leverage advanced cryptographic methods such as tokenization, format-preserving encryption, and quantum-resilient techniques to protect sensitive data. As a global leader in data security, our mission is to ensure that data isn’t just valuable but also usable, trusted, and safe.



Protegrity offers the opportunity to work at the intersection of innovation and collaboration, with the ability to make a meaningful impact on the industry while working alongside some of the brightest minds. Together, we are redefining how the world safeguards data, enabling organizations to thrive in a GenAI era where data is the ultimate currency. If you're ready to shape the future of data security, Protegrity is the place for you.



As an Associate Technical Writer! You'll create, update, and peer-review technical user guides, reference guides, programming guides, and all product documentation for our cutting-edge data security software. Provide editorial support for product descriptions, release notes, corporate security policies, performance reports, security reports, and white papers on various use cases of Protegrity products and data security technology solutions. If you have a strong technical background, excellent command of the English language, and a passion for writing and editing technical documentation, we want to hear from you.



Responsibilities:

 - Organise material, write, edit, and publish new and existing product documentation for a sophisticated security technology user audience.

 - Work with Documentation Manager to participate in efforts to peer review, restructure, optimise, improve, and expand the product documentation set.

 - Participate in efforts mentor junior writers in documentation activities.

 - Interpret product specifications and incorporate into product documentation.

 - Work with Product Management, R&D, and QA teams to gather documentation requirements.

 - Interview and interact with R&D and QA teams to interpret product specifications.

 - Gather information and research material to develop an in-depth knowledge of product technologies.

 - Create graphics and illustrations such as process and data flows.

 - Contribute content for presentations, white papers, web pages and any materials used for internal or external communication about Protegrity products.



Qualifications:

 - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science.

 - 0-2 years relevant experience writing highly technical software documentation targeted to audiences of various skill levels.

 - Strong attention to detail and ability to work independently.

 - Ability to understand and explain complex technical concepts in a clear and concise manner.

 - Ability to be immersed in the technology and very quickly develop product knowledge.

 - Excellent command of written and spoken English language, including software development vocabulary.

 - Exhibit excellent analytical and problem solving skills.

 - Familiarity with the markdown markup language.

 - Expert knowledge of the Microsoft Office suite.

 - Experience creating graphics using PowerPoint, Visio, SnagIT or other drawing tools.

 - Strong technical educational background, Software engineering or QA background, a big plus! experience with source control tools, such as, Subversion, GIT.

 - Experience using AI to generate documentation.



Desired Skills:

 - Prior experience in writing and editing product and service documentation for data security software products.

 - Experience in developing training materials for technology users.

 - Experience in working with offshore authors and engineers.

 - Experience with wikis and task tracking systems, ideally Confluence and JIRA.

 - Experience with bug tracking systems such as JIRA.

 - Familiarity with Agile methodologies and software development lifecycle.

source_jobs.title: Lead Full Stack Software Engineer 807
source_jobs.description:

We leverage advanced cryptographic methods such as tokenization, format-preserving encryption, and quantum-resilient techniques to protect sensitive data. About the Role



As a Lead Full Stack Engineer, you operate as a senior technical leader and delivery enabler across our visual tools, UI Shells, and SaaS-like capabilities in the Front-End organization. You own and evolve complex, high‑impact UI systems and related Back-End services while shaping the standards, workflows, and release practices that allow teams to deliver reliably, consistently, and at scale across multiple products and pipelines.



This role is front-end focused with strong openness to full stack capabilities where they meaningfully improve architecture, reliability, performance, and delivery velocity. You bring deep expertise in modern Front‑End systems and apply Back-End knowledge to influence API design, data flows, authentication, and system behavior in ways that result in more resilient and cohesive user experiences.



What You'll Do:

 - Serve as a core member of the centralized Front-End organization, driving consistent, scalable branding, interaction patterns, and visual identity across a global portfolio of security products.

 - Collaborate deeply with cross‑functional partners through engineering standups, sprint planning, design reviews, UX scrub sessions, demos, team forums, and 1:1s to ensure strong alignment, high quality, and predictable delivery.

 - Demonstrate curiosity and practical adoption of agent‑based and AI‑assisted development tools, using automation and intelligent workflows to streamline development, improve reliability, and shift focus toward high‑leverage architectural and product work.

 - Own and evolve shared Front‑End platforms and frameworks, driving architecture, infrastructure, and DevOps practices that enable reliable, scalable delivery across multiple products, pipelines, and teams.

 - Set and uphold a high bar for functional, intuitive user experiences, delivering successful end‑to‑end user journeys while maintaining excellence in UI quality, WCAG 2.2 Level AA Accessibility, performance, security, and visual consistency at scale.

 - Provide architectural leadership across UI and Back-End integration (APIs, auth, contracts, and operability) to ensure reliable UI solutions at scale.

 - Set direction for next‑generation UI architecture in cloud environments (Azure, AWS), aligning Front‑End systems with cloud‑native design patterns, modern security standards, and operational practices.

 - Identify and reduce friction across build, review, testing, and release workflows, driving durable improvements that enable predictable, low‑stress, repeatable release and operational practices at scale.



What You'll Need:

 - 6+ years of experience in modern Front‑End engineering (HTML5, CSS/SASS, React.js, TypeScript), with complementary full‑stack experience in Back-End services, and databases.

 - Strong foundation in computer science fundamentals, including data structures, algorithms, and system design.

 - Demonstrated knowledge and experience implementing client‑side security best practices to protect user data, application integrity, and trust.

source_jobs.title: Senior GenAI Backend Software Engineer (773)
source_jobs.description:
At Protegrity, we lead innovation by using AI and quantum-resistant cryptography to transform data protection across cloud-native, hybrid, on-premises, and open source environments. We leverage advanced cryptographic methods such as tokenization, format-preserving encryption, and quantum-resilient techniques to protect sensitive data. As a global leader in data security, our mission is to ensure that data isn’t just valuable but also usable, trusted, and safe.



Protegrity offers the opportunity to work at the intersection of innovation and collaboration, with the ability to make a meaningful impact on the industry while working alongside some of the brightest minds. Together, we are redefining how the world safeguards data, enabling organizations to thrive in a GenAI era where data is the ultimate currency. If you're ready to shape the future of data security, Protegrity is the place for you.


We are seeking a senior software engineer with experience in backend API development, Kubernetes-based microservices, Generative AI (GenAI) and Large Language Models (LLMs), along with a strong commitment to data security, privacy, and safety. This role offers the opportunity to build enterprise-grade capabilities that enable the safe, secure, and private consumption of GenAI by customers, as Protegrity accelerates its AI-driven strategy at a pivotal moment in the market.



Responsibilities:

 - Develop, test, and maintain robust container based microservice API architectures on Kubernetes clusters.

 - Design and develop high-quality, scalable software products supporting our customer’s security, safety, and privacy of GenAI and LLM-based systems with advanced guardrail capabilities.

 - Integrate GenAI systems including LLM, VectorDB, RAG pipelines, agents, and guardrails into the delivery of our product features.

 - Develop state of the art guardrails and systems leveraging agent workflows and tooling to support safety and security of GenAI.

 - Monitor, debug, and troubleshoot production issues.

 - Collaborate closely with other software engineers, product teams, AI researchers, and security specialists to deliver enterprise-grade software products and features for safe, secure, and private consumption of GenAI.

 - Evaluate and integrate open-source tools and third-party platforms to accelerate product development.



Requirements:

 - 10+ years of experience in backend software engineering with strong expertise in GenAI and LLM, and a focus on scaling of GenAI solutions.

 - Expertise developing and maintaining GenAI workflows including RAG workflows and prompt engineering techniques.

 - Experience developing and scaling cloud solutions using APIs, containers and microservice architectures with Kubernetes, Docker, Helm, etc.

 - Experience with integrating software products using authentication and authorization including cloud IAM, SSO, JWT, oAuth, etc.

 - Strong skills in Python with experience in cloud environments (e.g., AWS, Google Cloud, Azure).

 - Familiarity with version control tools, CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices.

 - Demonstrated ability to build and manage complex, distributed systems supporting enterprise solutions.



Should you accept this position, you will be required to consent to and successfully complete a background investigation. This may include, subject to local laws, verification of extended education and additional criminal and civil checks.



We offer a competitive salary and comprehensive benefits with generous vacation and holiday time off. All employees are also provided access to ongoing learning & development.

 

Ensuring a diverse and inclusive workplace is our priority. We are committed to an environment of acceptance where you are free to bring your full self to work. All qualified applicants and current employees will not be discriminated against on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, national origin, disability or veteran status.

source_jobs.title: Principal Customer Success Manager (817)
source_jobs.description:
At Protegrity, we lead innovation by using AI and quantum-resistant cryptography to transform data protection across cloud-native, hybrid, on-premises, and open source environments. We leverage advanced cryptographic methods such as tokenization, format-preserving encryption, and quantum-resilient techniques to protect sensitive data. As a global leader in data security, our mission is to ensure that data isn’t just valuable but also usable, trusted, and safe.



Protegrity offers the opportunity to work at the intersection of innovation and collaboration, with the ability to make a meaningful impact on the industry while working alongside some of the brightest minds. Together, we are redefining how the world safeguards data, enabling organizations to thrive in a GenAI era where data is the ultimate currency. If you're ready to shape the future of data security, Protegrity is the place for you.



Protegrity is seeking a passionate and talented Principal Customer Success Manager to join our Customer Success team.



This job is for you if

You are a senior, technically deep customer success leader who wants to operate where data protection, cloud-native platforms, and AI converge.



Protegrity’s customers — some of the world’s largest banks, insurers, healthcare systems, and retailers — are at an inflection point: they are moving from our proven appliance-based Data Security Platform (Enterprise Security Administrator, Data Security Gateway, and the classic protector family covering databases, applications, files, and big data) to a new, containerized, microservices-based architecture. At the same time, they are adopting Protegrity AI Team Edition to secure GenAI pipelines, RAG, and agentic workflows with semantic-preserving encryption, guardrails, anonymization, discovery, and synthetic data.



As a Principal Customer Success Manager, you will be the post-sales trusted advisor who guides these enterprises through that journey — protecting the value they already get from tokenization, format-preserving encryption, and field-level policy, while unlocking new AI use cases on the same policy fabric. You will champion customer-centric outcomes by working hand-in-hand with our architects, Support, Professional Services, Sales, and R&D to help customers build and execute roadmaps for comprehensive enterprise data security. If you want a role where your technical depth compounds, where you can lead conversations from a CISO’s office down to a platform engineer’s terminal, and where what you build today becomes the blueprint for how Fortune 500 customers protect data in the AI era — this is it.



Responsibilities

Responsibilities may include but are not limited to the following, organized around the Customer Success charter of retention, adoption, and advocacy:



Retention & Referenceability

 - Own a portfolio of strategic customer accounts as a book of business, with accountability for gross retention, net retention, renewal readiness, customer health, and long-term expansion across the installed base.

 - Manage to ARR retention targets by building account plans, identifying renewal risk early, and driving proactive engagement strategies that increase product adoption, strengthen executive alignment, and reinforce business value before renewal cycles begin.

 - Run structured at-risk account playbooks for customers showing signs of churn, including low platform usage, unresolved support escalations, stalled onboarding, weak executive sponsorship, limited use-case adoption, compliance gaps, or competitive displacement risk.

 - Work with customers and Protegrity architects to develop and refine solution plans for customer deployments, and assist in their implementation in an advisory capacity — spanning legacy ESA/DSG/protector environments, the new containerized microservices platform, and Protegrity AI Team Edition.

 - Help customers develop a roadmap for comprehensive data security across the enterprise — covering on-prem,

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Ontology & Reasoning Systems (819)
source_jobs.description:
At Protegrity, we lead innovation by using AI and quantum-resistant cryptography to transform data protection across cloud-native, hybrid, on-premises, and open source environments. We leverage advanced cryptographic methods such as tokenization, format-preserving encryption, and quantum-resilient techniques to protect sensitive data. As a global leader in data security, our mission is to ensure that data isn’t just valuable but also usable, trusted, and safe.



Protegrity offers the opportunity to work at the intersection of innovation and collaboration, with the ability to make a meaningful impact on the industry while working alongside some of the brightest minds. Together, we are redefining how the world safeguards data, enabling organizations to thrive in a GenAI era where data is the ultimate currency. If you're ready to shape the future of data security, Protegrity is the place for you.



Role Overview:

Protegrity is seeking a Senior Software Engineer, Ontology & Reasoning Systems to design and build the ontology and reasoning layer that connects structured sources, semi-structured metadata, and unstructured documents into a model that supports deterministic machine reasoning.



In this role, you will formalize complex domains into entities, relationships, constraints, and rules that allow the system to derive conclusions from underlying facts. A key focus will be making those conclusions explainable and traceable, with clear derivation paths back to the source data and logic that produced them.



LLMs play an important role at the ingestion boundary, supporting extraction, normalization, and categorization of information from unstructured or ambiguous inputs. The reasoning layer itself must remain deterministic, auditable, and explainable, with appropriate checks on model-generated outputs before they are used as trusted facts.



You will work closely with product and research teams to define formal models, improve reasoning quality, and build systems that support accurate, reviewable outputs. This role is well suited for someone with a strong foundation in logic, graph-based systems, formal modeling, and production-grade software engineering.



What You’ll Do:

 - Own the ontology and rule layer that unifies structured, semi-structured, and unstructured enterprise data into a single reasoning model.

 - Model entities, relationships, events, and constraints across heterogeneous sources, and develop rules that derive conclusions from combinations of facts.

 - Use LLMs at the ingestion boundary for extraction, normalization, and categorization, with appropriate validation of model-generated outputs.

 - Make system findings traceable by connecting each conclusion to the source facts and rules that produced it.

 - Build retrieval capabilities, including vector search, keyword search, and graph traversal, to support reasoning workflows.

 - Improve quality through entity resolution, constraint modeling, and structured handling of conflicting or overlapping sources.

 - Ship production services and research prototypes with a focus on correctness, reliability, and maintainability.
   
   

What You’ll Need:

 - 5+ years of experience building backend systems in a modern language such as Python, Go, Java, Scala, Rust, or similar, or equivalent practical experience.

 - A strong formal and mathematical foundation, including discrete mathematics, logic, and graph theory.

 - The ability to reason about soundness, completeness, and tractability, and to express complex domains as formal rules and constraints.

 - Hands-on experience with declarative, logic-based, or rule-based reasoning systems such as Datalog, Answer Set Programming, constraint logic programming, Prolog, production rule engines, SMT solvers, or similar.

 - Practical experience building LLM-powered systems, including structured extraction, RAG, or classification pipelines, with sound judgment about where model outputs require validation.

 - Deep exper
